title: Helenï¿½s Tower
content:
Helenï¿½s tower, here I stand,
Dominant over sea and land.
Sonï¿½s love built me, and I hold
Motherï¿½s love in letterï¿½d gold.
Love is in and out of time,
I am mortal stone and lime.
Would my granite girth were strong
As either love, to last as long
I should wear my crown entire
To and throï¿½ the Doomsday fire,
And be found of angel eyes
In earthï¿½s recurring Paradise.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alfred Lord Tennyson, titled "Helenï¿½s Tower", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
